title: Immunohistochemistry and Imaging of Human Midbrain Organoid Sections
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"a2slj","text":"This protocol aims to perform immunohistochemistry on human brain organoid sections to visualize specific antigens.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"rm4l","text":"Handle all reagents, especially PFA and Triton X-100, with appropriate personal protective equipment (PPE). Work in a fume hood when using PFA.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text:
{"blocks":[{"key":"cel7m","text":"Reagents:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"emafv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eh1kb","text":"•	4% paraformaldehyde (PFA) in PBS (freshly prepared or validated stock)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9i9mb","text":"•	Phosphate buffered saline (PBS), sterile","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cpof6","text":"•	30% sucrose in PBS (cryoprotectant)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"abacj","text":"•	OCT embedding compound","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"34vle","text":"•	Sodium citrate buffer (pH 6.0) for antigen retrieval","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8151k","text":"•	Triton X-100 (or equivalent detergent)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1tdor","text":"•	Normal donkey serum or normal goat serum (blocking)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1l8s1","text":"•	Primary antibodies (see your internal list for specific targets)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ev6c3","text":"•	Species-appropriate fluorescent secondary antibodies (Alexa Fluor or equivalent)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6661f","text":"•	DAPI nuclear stain (1 µM working concentration)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e6ciq","text":"•	Mounting medium compatible with fluorescence imaging (e.g., anti-fade)


","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Gently transfer organoids to a tube containing 4% PFA and incubate 1 hour at room temperature with gentle rocking. Remove fixative and wash organoids 3× with PBS (5 min each). Transfer organoids to 30% sucrose in PBS and keep overnight at 4 °C until organoids sink (cryoprotection). Embed organoids in OCT in cryomolds; snap-freeze on dry ice or in a cold isopentane bath, then store blocks at −80 °C until sectioning.
Equilibrate OCT blocks in the cryostat chamber at the recommended temperature, between -22 °C and -14 °C. Cut 20 μm sections and mount onto Superfrost Plus slides.
Air-dry sections for approximately 10 minutes at room temperature to ensure adhesion.
Prewarm sodium citrate buffer (pH 6.0) or EDTA-antigen-retrieval solution (pH 8.0) to ~85 °C. Submerge slides in buffer and perform antigen retrieval for 20 minutes.
Remove slides and allow to cool to room temperature (20 min). Wash slides 3 times in PBS/TBS (5 minutes each).
Prepare blocking buffer: 10% normal donkey/goat serum in PBS/TBS containing 0.2% Triton X-100. Incubate sections in blocking buffer for 1 hour at room temperature.
Dilute primary antibodies in 5% blocking buffer. Apply primary antibody solution to sections and incubate overnight at 4 °C in a humidified chamber.
Wash slides 3 times with PBS/TBS (5 minutes each) at room temperature.
Dilute species-appropriate fluorescent secondary antibodies in PBS/TBS or blocking buffer. Incubate sections with secondary antibodies for 2 hours at room temperature in the dark.
Incubate sections with DAPI (1 µM) for 5 minutes at room temperature. Wash once with PBS (5 minutes). Mount coverslips using fluorescence-compatible mounting medium.
Store slides at 4 °C protected from light for short-term; for long-term storage, keep sealed at -20 °C if compatible with antibodies used.

This protocol describes paraformaldehyde fixation, cryosectioning at 20 μm, antigen retrieval, blocking/permeabilization, primary/secondary antibody incubations. Perform all steps using sterile technique where appropriate. Prepare fresh 4% paraformaldehyde (PFA) or use properly stored PFA fixative. Work in a fume hood for fixation. Pre-chill cryoprotectant (30% sucrose) and OCT embedding medium. Confirm antibody species and appropriate secondary antibodies prior to starting. For phosphorylated protein targets, use TBS instead of PBS in all steps.
creator: Hariam Raji
